clinical_coherence.notes: 40F with fibromyalgia, chronic pain, anxiety, depression, childhood physical abuse, and extensive comorbidities. Progressive bilateral weakness with wheelchair dependence over 8 months. Equivocal Hoover sign initially, distractible tremor with entrainment, give-way weakness, non-anatomic sensory loss. Complex pain overlay with fibromyalgia and chronic back pain creating diagnostic anchoring challenges. Psychosocial stressors (husband job loss, mother's cancer) realistic.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG with captured events showing no ictal correlate. Brain and spine MRI normal (mild lumbar disc desiccation as red herring). EMG/NCS normal. Labs normal except mildly low vitamin D. All outputs concordant with FND and appropriately exclude organic etiologies.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD F44.4 correct. Excellent differential including MS, myelopathy, MG, and CIDP. Red herrings well-designed: MS aunt, lumbar disc changes, fibromyalgia attribution, low vitamin D, equivocal Hoover sign.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Diagnostic puzzle is perfectly calibrated. Equivocal Hoover sign, wheelchair dependence making gait assessment difficult, complex pain comorbidities creating diagnostic anchoring, extensive negative workup needed, and multiple red herrings. This would genuinely challenge a clinician.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 40F with fibromyalgia, chronic pain, extensive medication list (duloxetine, pregabalin, cyclobenzaprine, sumatriptan), and complex psychosocial stressors is highly realistic. Real estate agent on disability leave is plausible.

red_herring_quality.notes: Excellent red herrings: maternal aunt with MS, lumbar disc desiccation on MRI, fibromyalgia as attribution bias, low vitamin D, equivocal Hoover sign, and complex pain history creating anchoring bias. All are realistic and well-justified.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding diagnostic puzzle teaching FND recognition in the context of complex comorbid pain syndromes. Demonstrates how fibromyalgia and chronic pain can mask or overlap with functional weakness. Teaches the importance of distractibility and entrainment testing when Hoover sign is equivocal.

strengths: Exceptionally well-designed diagnostic puzzle with realistic complexity
Multiple high-quality red herrings that challenge diagnostic reasoning
Complex pain-FND overlap that mirrors real clinical challenges
Equivocal positive signs requiring advanced examination techniques
Comprehensive follow-up outputs spanning multiple domains
